results.title: AI/ML API Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
4.0

Based on 14 reviews

Review AI/ML API?

Reviewers mostly praise AI/ML API for reducing integration friction: one endpoint, broad model access, easy docs, predictable behavior under load, and fast support make it useful for prototyping and, for some, stable enough for production. Several also like the unified API for comparing models and moving OpenAI-style projects over quickly. But the feedback is not uniformly positive. A minority report serious reliability and billing problems, including unavailable models, frequent errors, misleading pricing, and disputed charges, so sentiment is favorable overall but clearly mixed on trust and consistency.

+11

Summarized with AI

extensive model library(6) [...] We're experienced developers - we know when the problem is on their end Payment system designed to fail, forcing you to enable auto-topup Each failed click triggers backend queries that authorize MULTIPLE payments instead of single payment Once they get your money, good luck getting refunds! Multiple unauthorized charges after signing up for simple pay-as-you-go plan Services never functioned despite charges Months of ignored refund requests No legitimate business information or physical address Suspiciously generic 5-star reviews with zero technical details Customer support only responds to public complaints, ignores private tickets When we posted factual reviews, they immediately tried to bribe us to remove reviews in exchange for refunds they'd been ignoring for months. Classic scammer [...] responsive support (4)

•1 review

Tried a bunch of different AI platforms, but AIMLAPI has honestly been one of the smoothest experiences. There are tons of great models to choose from, setup is super easy, and the documentation is very easy to read unlike Openrouter's. I also really like the token-based pricing which is really convenient. Big shoutout to the support team, probably the quickest and most helpful support I’ve ever dealt with

results.title: Predictive AI (2026): Compare the Best | Product Hunt
results.content:
# Predictive AI (2026): Compare the Best

Predictive AI tools analyze patterns to forecast outcomes. This category unites language models, fast inference, search, and analytics for research, trading, and marketing.

Gemini
Groq Chat
Image Object Removal API
Hume AI
Wope
Steer
Wispr Flow: Dictation That Works Everywhere
Promoted

## Top reviewed predictive AI products [...] The pattern is clear: shipping AI as a new app is the slow path. The fast path is grafting onto a surface the user already touches. The cost of building a standalone AI app dropped 90%+. The cost of getting it noticed did not. Surface integration sidesteps the noticing problem because the surface already has users.

Janice
AdFox (formerly GoodsFox)

AdFox (formerly GoodsFox)

Product Hunt is home to amazing products across every category.

Today, we wanted to look at AI creative tools from a slightly different angle not just features, but the real-world marketing activity behind them.

Ashish Mishra [...] These steps surface drift, quantify impact, and prevent bad models from fully rolling out.

Tullie Murrell
Tullie Murrell
MindReader v1
nybl
Flywheel.cx
Imed Radhouani
Rankfender

Rankfender

Last week, six AI products launched on Product Hunt that share one move. None of them ask users to open a new app. They embed into surfaces people already touch.

Hardware: Dune Keypad (46 upvotes) sits next to your keyboard with Claude integration. Video calls: Mina Meeting Assistant (47 upvotes). Text threads: folk (51 upvotes). Chat windows: Databox MCP (39 upvotes) plugs business data into Claude via Model Context Protocol. Mac autocomplete: Typeahead (22 upvotes).

results.title: AdCreative.ai Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Instead of starting from scratch, I can quickly generate multiple creatives and test them right away. It’s especially useful when campaigns start to fatigue and you need fresh variations fast. Some outputs feel a bit generic, but a few are surprisingly good and only need minor tweaks before launching.

Overall, it’s a solid tool for saving time and scaling creative testing without relying too much on manual design work.

#### What needs improvement

There are a few areas that could be improved:

Some creatives feel repetitive if you generate multiple batches

Better control over brand voice and style would help

The scoring system doesn’t always match real performance

Would love to see stronger support for video creatives [...] In fact, I am actively recommending people NOT work with them because I'm still waiting for the refund I had to fight for 2 weeks ago and they were unapologetic, unhelpful and predatory.

#### What needs improvement

Be ethical. Fix your email communication system. Follow your own policies. Don't be a predator.

Fernando Monfort

#### What needs improvement
